title: Linux Fragnesia 'Dirty Frag' Varient
Comment:
Status as of 5/14 

Current Condition: high-severity local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability affecting the Linux kernel’s XFRM ESP-in-TCP/IPsec subsystem. The flaw allows an unprivileged local attacker to corrupt kernel page-cache memory and deterministically escalate privileges to root by abusing memory-fragment handling behavior in the networking stack. Researchers have described the issue as a more reliable evolution of prior Linux page-cache exploitation techniques because it does not rely heavily on race conditions, increasing exploitation stability across affected systems. Public proof-of-concept code

Actions: Add to watchlist to monitor for patching

Needs: Patches and confrimation of configuration impact
